diagrams.title: Closure Properties of Regular Languages
diagrams.description: This diagram illustrates the operations under which regular languages are closed, showing that applying union, intersection, complement, concatenation, Kleene star, homomorphism, or inverse homomorphism to regular languages produces another regular language.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental closure properties that define regular languages and their behavior under various language operations

diagrams.title: Pumping Lemma Visualization
diagrams.description: This diagram visualizes the Pumping Lemma concept, showing how a sufficiently long string in a regular language can be divided into three parts (xyz), where the middle portion (y) can be pumped (repeated) to generate more strings that remain in the language.
elements: Division into x, y, z parts
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the Pumping Lemma used to prove that certain languages are not regular by showing the pumping property that all regular languages must satisfy

diagrams.title: Decision Properties of Regular Languages
diagrams.description: This diagram shows the algorithmic decision properties for regular languages, including emptiness testing (does the language contain any strings), finiteness testing (is the language finite), membership testing (is a specific string in the language), and equivalence testing (are two regular languages the same).
diagrams.purpose: Explains what properties of regular languages can be algorithmically decided and how these decision procedures work
